name: Kentucky Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Kentucky Board of SLP and Audiology

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Kentucky SLP license cost?
Is Kentucky part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Kentucky allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Kentucky have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Kentucky participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Kentucky without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Kentucky?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
